evals.prompt: Our exit-intent popup offers a 10% discount coupon and converts at 2.1%. Competitors seem to be getting 5-8% on exit popups. What should we test to improve this?
evals.expected_output: CRO analysis of the exit popup with specific A/B test hypotheses covering: offer type, headline, urgency/scarcity, design, and targeting improvements.
assertions: Questions whether discount is the right offer vs. alternative (guide, trial, demo)
Provides at least 3 specific A/B test hypotheses
Addresses the headline and value prop of the popup
Covers targeting refinement (who sees it, when)
References industry benchmarks for exit popup conversion rates

evals.prompt: When should a popup appear — on load, after X seconds, on scroll, or on exit intent?
evals.expected_output: Data-driven guidance on popup timing for different goals (email capture, discount, demo booking) with recommendations per traffic source and page type.
assertions: Distinguishes timing recommendations by goal (email vs. purchase vs. demo)
Covers scroll depth triggers as often better than time-based
Addresses exit intent as high-intent targeting
Notes that on-load popups have high close rates
Gives a specific recommendation for the most common use case

evals.prompt: Design the visual layout of my popup modal
evals.expected_output: Offers CRO-focused design principles for popup layout (visual hierarchy, button placement, close button), notes that visual design is better handled by a design skill.
assertions: Provides CRO-relevant design principles (button color, CTA placement, close button visibility)
Notes that detailed visual design is beyond scope of CRO optimization
